reasoning: Data released up through mid-2025 confirms that Nigeria did not achieve its goal of 95% financial inclusion—much less bank account ownership—before 2025. According to the EFInA Access to Financial Services (A2F) 2023 Survey, overall financial inclusion was just 74% with only 52% of Nigerian adults relying specifically on banking services. Further reporting in July 2025 unequivocally states that Nigeria fell short of the CBN's 95% inclusion target for 2024. Consequently, only about 60 million individuals had Bank Verification Numbers by late 2023, leaving almost half the adult population without any bank account. Since 95% bank account ownership is mathematically and practically impossible given this data, the probability resolves to the lowest allowable boundary of 2%.
